title: ScrollArea Component
content:
## ScrollArea Component The `ScrollArea` component provides a customizable and accessible scrolling experience for content within your application. It's built on top of `@radix-ui/react-scroll-area` and integrates with the DocMed UI library's styling and utility functions. ### Purpose This component is designed to replace native browser scrollbars with a more aesthetically pleasing and controllable scrollable area. It allows for vertical and horizontal scrolling with custom scrollbars. ### Key Exports * **`ScrollArea`**: The main component for creating a scrollable container. It accepts standard HTML attributes and props from `@radix-ui/react-scroll-area`'s `Root` component. * **`ScrollBar`**: A sub-component used internally by `ScrollArea` to render the scrollbar. It can be customized with specific `orientation` and class names. ### Props **`ScrollArea` Props:** * `className` (string): Custom class names to apply to the scroll area container. * `children` (React.ReactNode): The content to be displayed within the scrollable area. * `...props`: Any other props accepted by `@radix-ui/react-scroll-area`'s `Root` component. **`ScrollBar` Props:** * `className` (string): Custom class names to apply to the scrollbar. * `orientation` (string): Specifies the orientation of the scrollbar, either `'vertical'` (default) or `'horizontal'`. * `...props`: Any other props accepted by `@radix-ui/react-scroll-area`'s `ScrollAreaScrollbar` component. ### Usage Example ```tsx import { ScrollArea } from '@/components/ui/scroll-area'; function MyComponent() { return (
{/* Long content that will overflow */}

This is some content.

This is some content.

This is some content.

This is some content.

This is some content.

This is some content.

This is some content.

This is some content.

This is some content.

This is some content.

This is some content.

This is some content.
); } ``` This example creates a `ScrollArea` within a fixed-size container. If the content inside `ScrollArea` exceeds the container's dimensions, native scrollbars will appear, styled by the `ScrollArea` and `ScrollBar` components.
